steps.title: Read Schmidt's actions
steps.content: Schmidt reacts to the wife's comment by wincing, sighing dramatically, and making an obviously false excuse to leave. These actions show his disapproval without him directly saying that he disapproves.

steps.title: Reject invented details
steps.content: Choice A makes an unsupported claim that he behaves theatrically only with these people. Choice B treats his false excuse as genuine absentmindedness. Choice C invents an earlier disagreement about a particular painting. The answer is D.
